DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charlotte Kernan, Esq. 415-936-6449 Plaintiff in pro Per.
Ryan Stevens, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3975 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views and the technical issues. Plaintiff Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendants City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. It is undisputed that the filing requirements of the Government Claims Act apply to plaintiffs' negligence cause of action against the City. (Gov't Code 905, 905.2, 945.4.) The City's records from the Claims Division show that no claim was filed. The court takes judicial notice of the Rothschild declaration pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(c) and Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 376.

Plaintiffs allege that they sent a letter with their claim to Susan Ehrlich, CEO of the Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital, and a number of physicians. However, the Government Claims Act requires that a claim be actually received by "the clerk, secretary, auditor, or board" of the local public entity, and the San Francisco Administrative Code provides that the claim must be delivered to the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors or Controller of the City and County. (Gov't Code 915(e)(1); SF Admin. Code 10.20-4.) "The goals of the claims statutes are to provide entities with sufficient information to investigate and appropriately resolve claims and to plan for potential liabilities. These goals are satisfied when an adequate claim is actually received by an appropriate entity representative. The Legislature retains authority to determine which representatives are appropriate." (DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal.4th 983, 994.) Thus, unless Plaintiffs can allege in good faith that their claim was actually received by one of the statutorily designated recipients within the time prescribed for presentation, the demurrer will be sustained without leave to amend. (See id. at 992-993 [holding that a misdirected claim fails to comply with the statute, and will satisfy the presentation requirement only if it is actually received by a statutorily designated recipient].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
